summary: Hunter/Seamons matrix delivers crystal‑clear sound, showcasing a legendary 1978 show with a blistering Peggy‑O and fire‑hot Fire.
review: The matrix cut by Hunter (Seamons) is superb – every note of Garcia’s soaring solo on Peggy‑O and Weir’s clean Ibanez tone comes through beautifully, while the audience ambience adds vivid atmosphere. Musically the band is locked in from mid‑Jack Straw onward, delivering a barn‑burner Fire, a tight Good Ship of Fools and an inspired Scarlet Begonias despite a few vocal scat critiques.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer stars (mostly 5) and glowing comments about both performance and the matrix recording indicate an exceptional show with excellent audio quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hunter/Seamons matrix praised as ‘superb’ and ‘fantastic’; clear instrument separation (Garcia, Weir), balanced audience blend, no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set launches fast and rock‑in’; second set builds into classic 1978 jams, maintaining momentum despite a formulaic feel that doesn’t detract from the overall excitement.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solo on Peggy‑O called ‘the best ever’; his tone, phrasing and vocal improvisation on Fire highlighted as outstanding.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s clean Ibanez sound praised; noted as a rare moment when his guitar is fully audible and shines.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey (Hart) on left and Bill (Kreutzmann) on right are well balanced in the matrix mix.
